focus: Iteration 10 final consolidation: close Q1-Q6, operator runbook, durable prevention fixes, and residual risks for synthesis.

graphEvents.label: Q1-Q6 have crisp evidence-cited final answers
Operator runbook reconciles vector-present stale rows before daemon drain
Reindex success metadata commit is the single fix that would have prevented the observed no-success-count-rise incident
Retention and daemon config fixes prevent re-parking and stale-env recurrence

label: Pending rows become failed through max-age retention first and pending-cap overflow second
evidence: enforceRetryRetentionLimits updates pending/retry rows to failed for max age, then selects pending/retry overflow past maxPending and marks those failed.

label: Retry-manager can embed clean pending rows, but retention runs before queue selection
evidence: getRetryQueue calls enforceRetryRetentionLimits before selecting pending/retry rows; isEligibleForRetry returns true for pending.

label: embedder_set reindex writes vectors and flips active pointer without committing memory_index success
evidence: reindex writes vector tables then completion transaction calls setActiveEmbedder and setJobStatus only; dist reindex.js:316-318 is the durable-fix anchor.

label: Operator repair must reconcile vector-present stale metadata before any drain of missing-vector rows
evidence: Iteration 7 measured all 17326 non-success rows with active vec_768 and vec_memories coverage; iteration 8 defined dry-run/apply buckets.

label: Snapshot counts drift, so runbook must use live preflight counts and dry-run buckets
evidence: Prompt baseline was success 9623 / total 26967; iteration 7 reported success 9652 / total 26978; iteration 10 read-only spot check showed success 9652 with pending/retry split drift.

label: memory_embedding_reconcile must resolve and verify the active shard dynamically before mutating rows
Retention must not fail never-attempted clean pending rows

direction: treat current backlog as primarily missing embeddings
reason: Validated active-vector coverage shows the current backlog is primarily stale metadata, not absent vectors.
